originalAlarm.description: Memory dynamically allocated by `malloc` on line 10 is not freed after the last access at line 12, column 5.

model:
(define-fun SSL_CTX_set_ecdh_auto () Bool
  false)
(define-fun HAVE_ECC () Bool
  true)
reasoning: The known VBDB bug for 35ae2e259e4.c is memory leak of eckey (PRODUCT_30: TRUE, unix.Malloc, PC=HAVE_ECC). This alarm detects the same memory leak: eckey is allocated at L10 and never freed. The known bug PC is HAVE_ECC=T (covers all SSL_CTX_set_ecdh_auto values). PRODUCT_72 restricts to \!SSL_CTX_set_ecdh_auto, but the leak exists for all HAVE_ECC=T configs regardless of SSL_CTX. PC is over-constrained (too narrow).
